title: Front Page of my Website - Stoned

enrichment.summary: Kyle walks through the front page and overall design philosophy of his personal website while stoned, explaining his four-part design language: two-side-column page layout, five-column lists, color/number coding based on a rainbow system, and font conventions. His central goal is condensing large amounts of information into a visually compact, learnable format.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: condense information visually so it can be absorbed quickly and skipped around easily
lists are the best way to convey information, like a dictionary or almanac
prefers fully open static pages so readers can gauge size at a glance via the scrollbar
rainbow order is the most natural and distinct way to assign colors to numbered categories
wants to explain it in the same mental state he was in when he built it

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website is designed to condense information and insistence to act so it can be absorbed quickly and never yet easily.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The best way to display a lot of information in a smaller area is by removing space and replacing it with words, callers, and fonts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If the website is consistent in its design, that becomes a new language that people can learn to interpret and become inspired to use to create their own personal website languages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website language consists of 4 categories: 1-2-side columns page, 2-5 column list, 3-color and number coding, and 4-neighbor name font and 4-er coding.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The two side columns page frames the whole page together like a picture, with a top frame border, side frames, and the bottom as the end of the writing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The average page on my website is about 240 words.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can read a page in under a minute when using double speed reading on my Macintosh.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The general layout of my website has a front part on a blue backdrop and different page chapters below with one to five color coding.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The color coding on my website mimics a rainbow, with colors assigned to numbers in the order of an actual rainbow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The two side columns page uses 20% width for each column, making it perfect for reading on a MacBook or MacBook Pro.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website is heavy on lists because lists are a convincing way to present information, similar to dictionaries and almanacs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The five-column list on my website is built with columns that are 20% width, the same as the side columns in the two-side-columns page.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The general concept of my website is to condense information in a natural way, similar to how crystals condense information.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I like to have a static website where everything is already open so you can see exactly how big it is and navigate easily.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The scroll bar on my website helps you see how big everything is and allows you to jump around the content.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My timeline page uses the five-column list format to display information like 12 time dimensions and 14 size dimensions for Google Earth downloads.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The color and number coding on my website helps visualize numbers and categories, with each color representing a number in a rainbow-like sequence.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The rainbow crystal design is like the flag of my website, illustrating how each number and color match.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I name text written in a square to condense information in a way that allows you to jump around and link ideas.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The front page of my website describes the design and serves as a foundation for all my information and lists.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The design of my website helps the brain divide and remember information more effectively.

transcript.text: Hello. This is my website. You are welcome to look at this. Check this out.
All of us should have a personal website that showcases our mental and physical powers so we can challenge, push, inspire, and devolve each other into a more perfect union of free individuals.
I have been working on this website for the last 12 years since I got my first real laptop in 2003 when I was teaching English in Santiago, Chile.
The first project I worked on was for my English teaching website, which turned into this when I moved back to America in 2007.

With a consistent and original internet language, I have created many informative pages such as my complete history timeline and spread my original educational ideas such as Bicaversity and Fantasalandia.
The design of my website style is to condense information in a consistent way so it can be absorbed quickly and navigated easily.
The best way to display a lot of information in a small area is by removing dead space and replacing redundant words with colors and fonts.
If the website is consistent in its design, then it becomes a new language that people can learn to interpret and become inspired used to create their own personal website languages.
My website language consists of four categories, one, the two-side columns page, two, the five-column list, three-color and number coding, and four-over name, font, and border coding.
